mission.title: Install and Configure Vultr Kubernetes Engine (VKE)
mission.description: This mission guides you through installing the Vultr Kubernetes Engine (VKE), a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on Vultr infrastructure. It is ideal for users looking for a straightforward way to leverage Kubernetes without the overhead of managing the underlying infrastructure. Key trade-offs include reliance on Vultr's cloud services and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the Vultr CLI
steps.description:
The Vultr CLI is required to interact with the Vultr API for creating and managing Kubernetes clusters.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/vultr/vultr-cli/releases/download/v1.0.0/vultr-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf vultr-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv vultr-cli /usr/local/bin/

vultr-cli version

```

> Download the latest version of the Vultr CLI.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the CLI binary to a directory in your PATH.
> Verify the installation by checking the version.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster on Vultr with the specified version and node count.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s create --region 'ewr' --version '1.30' --node-count 3 --plan 'vc2-2c-4gb'

```

> Replace 'ewr' with your preferred region and adjust the plan as needed.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s kubeconfig --cluster-id <your-cluster-id> > ~/.kube/config

export KUBECONFIG=~/.kube/config

```

> Replace <your-cluster-id> with the ID of your created cluster.
> Set the KUBECONFIG environment variable.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for your cluster to ensure optimal performance.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/master/cluster-autoscaler/cloud-provider/vultr/examples/cluster-autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/monitoring/main/monitoring.yaml

```

> This applies the cluster autoscaler configuration.
> This applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command will delete the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s delete --cluster-id <your-cluster-id>

```

> Replace <your-cluster-id> with the ID of your cluster.

uninstall.title: Remove Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
This step ensures that any orphaned resources such as load balancers and persistent volumes are cleaned up.

```bash

vultr-cli loadbalancer list

vultr-cli loadbalancer delete --loadbalancer-id <lb-id>

```

> List load balancers to identify any that need to be deleted.
> Replace <lb-id> with the ID of the load balancer to delete.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. This step will upgrade your cluster to the latest version.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s backup --cluster-id <your-cluster-id>

vultr-cli k8s upgrade --cluster-id <your-cluster-id> --version '1.30'

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup your cluster before proceeding.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that all nodes are in 'Ready' state after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster: Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the selected plan does not have enough resources available.

**Fix:**
Choose a different plan with more resources or reduce the node count.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubeconfig not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig file is not set up correctly or is missing.

**Fix:**
Re-run the kubeconfig setup command and ensure the file is created at the correct path.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl get nodes returns no nodes'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not be fully initialized or there may be a connectivity issue.

**Fix:**
Check the cluster status using 'vultr-cli k8s list' and ensure your kubeconfig is correct.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'LoadBalancer service not provisioned'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the cloud controller manager is not configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the Vultr Cloud Controller Manager is deployed and configured properly.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Vultr instance types and improved load balancer provisioning.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for older instance types.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.29, ensure that your configurations are compatible with the new instance types.

resolution.summary:
You should see output similar to the following when everything is working: 'NAME                STATUS   ROLES    AGE   VERSION
node-1              Ready    <none>   10m   v1.30.0
node-2              Ready    <none>   10m   v1.30.0
node-3              Ready    <none>   10m   v1.30.0'

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools installed and sufficient resources available.
